title: Rytigynia orbicularis
intro: Rytigynia orbicularis, also known as the African ironwood, is a species of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family, commonly found in tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Rytigynia orbicularis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Rytigynia orbicularis is classified as a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The genus Rytigynia belongs to the tribe Vanguerieae. Rytigynia orbicularis is native to tropical Africa. It is a shrub or small tree species.
uses:
Rytigynia orbicularis is reported to have medicinal properties. 
Its leaves are used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including treating fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. 
In some regions, it is used as a remedy for stomach problems and inflammation. 
More research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential health benefits.
